notes: Fixed June-end (and therefore July-start) savings being overstated. When an investment contribution is funded directly FROM savings (a journal investment_contrib with accountId=savings, e.g. reinvesting RD redemption proceeds into the FD), that cash leaves the savings pool — but the projected savings chain wasn't subtracting it, so savings was inflated by that amount (here ~1,58,000: redemption raised surplus via the income fold, but the matching FD outflow from savings was never netted). Fix: the projection-sheet savings residue (_plannedTargetForFuture) now subtracts savings-funded investment contributions for the month. The Net Worth savings chain (projectSavingsChain.residueForMonth), which is built on the engine surplus that excludes real savings-side flows entirely, now nets BOTH redemptions into savings (+) and contributions funded from savings (−). Net effect: closing RD and reinvesting into FD now correctly leaves savings ~flat (money moved RD→savings→FD), and June-end / July-start savings reconcile to live balance + true residue.
